decision_scope: First diagnostic catalog semantics and v0.4 compatibility mapping inputs only

public_checks.capability: Boundedly acquire and parse requested adoption-policy and register documents without deriving identity from parser prose.
Evaluate a safely parsed current adoption declaration against the accepted adoption schema contract.
Evaluate declared-profile validity, exclusivity, and spec-derived effective-profile semantics.
Evaluate the selected split or lite layout and its profile-compatible structural obligations.
Compare the declared profile release with the pinned checkout release identity.
Compare the declared profile commit and consumed trusted resources with the pinned checkout commit.
Establish that schemas, validator, and trusted profile resources come from one bounded pinned checkout.
Evaluate lexical and resolved containment of adopter-owned policy and security paths.
Establish deterministic presence and minimum usable content of profile-required adopter artifacts.
Evaluate safely loaded claim, invariant, defeater, and residual registers against their selected schemas.
Evaluate cross-register IDs, references, grounds, intent, and minimum evidence semantics.
Evaluate the declared specification workflow and bounded adopter-owned entry-document root.
Evaluate the visible root-guide assurance reading order required for adopters and agents.
Evaluate component routing-map shape and invariant-reference integrity.
Evaluate the mechanically decidable obligations of the declared adoption stage.
Evaluate the structural presence, bounded path, and non-empty repository record declared for human review.
Evaluate mechanically decidable date, URL, ordering, and non-future review-record relationships.
Evaluate the declared private-security and artifact-backed issue-integration controls.
Evaluate the mechanical archived-profile structure while reporting the limit on verification of historical facts.
Report structural provenance for review of the primary current or head profile classification without claiming classification correctness.
Boundedly acquire and classify transition-specific current, base, and auxiliary policy inputs.
Classify whether a trusted caller event supplies a single pull-request review transition and its required revision identities.
Evaluate base-to-head declaration changes that weaken, reclassify, or move reviewed assurance policy.
Evaluate lexical and resolved base-to-head bindings of the adoption declaration and mapped policy targets.

authority_refs.locator: reviewed policy path and containment boundary

public_checks.capability: Evaluate base-to-head claim, invariant, defeater, and residual changes that weaken recorded assurance state or human grounds.
Evaluate whether each touched mapped component is addressed by assurance evidence or a valid explicit impact declaration.
Evaluate bounded, lossless, canonical repository-relative changed-file input.
Evaluate the bounded leading PR directive block and assurance-diff evidence used for impact and policy-change routing.
Evaluate the central profile release VERSION syntax and selected release identity.
Evaluate bundled policy schemas as valid, offline JSON Schema draft 2020-12 resources.
Evaluate bundled split and lite templates against their schemas and minimum starting-path obligations.
Evaluate synchronized agent-instruction blocks and their canonical assurance reading order.
Evaluate that the shipped CODEOWNERS template covers every default adopted policy location.
Evaluate the selected central profile release tree after its version, schema, template, instruction, ownership, and remaining repository invariants are checked.
Establish the selected adopter bundle's aggregate mechanically decidable conformance after every applicable first-revision adoption capability has reported its own state.
Evaluate repository-wide release hygiene invariants that are independent of version, schema, template, instruction, and ownership conformance.

allocated_entries.semantic_condition: The selected archived structure is mechanically checkable, but the truth of its historical facts is not mechanically verified.

default_display.title: Archived historical facts are not mechanically verified

default_display.title: Touched component is not routed to assurance evidence

allocated_entries.semantic_condition: The adoption declaration lacks a usable upstream version value.
The declared upstream version differs from the version recorded by the pinned profile checkout.
The adoption declaration lacks a usable upstream commit value.
The declared upstream commit differs from the commit at the pinned profile checkout's HEAD.
The trusted validation resources actually consumed are not exactly the regular, unmodified resources recorded at the pinned profile checkout's HEAD.
A trusted validation resource is hidden from ordinary worktree comparison by an assume-unchanged or skip-worktree index flag.
The schema directory and pinned profile checkout do not resolve to the same trusted profile tree.
A declared policy artifact path is not a repository-relative path contained within the adopting repository.
A declared policy artifact path contains a prohibited control character.
A declared adopter-controlled policy artifact resolves inside trusted or otherwise non-adopter data.
A declared policy artifact path traverses an absolute symlink or a symlink that escapes and re-enters the repository.
A declared policy artifact path traverses an in-repository symlink whose lexical path, resolved target, and target parent all require review protection.

default_display.title: policy path in project symlink review warning

allocated_entries.semantic_condition: An assurance artifact required by the declared layout and profiles is absent from its effective path.
An assurance artifact required at its effective path is not a readable regular file.
A required textual assurance artifact is empty, whitespace-only, or contains no visible meaningful content.
A lite-layout adoption provides neither a nonempty inline system description nor a usable system-description file.
A trust-critical adoption has no defeater register at its effective path.
An assurance register does not conform to the schema for its register kind.
A profile-required assurance register contains no usable entry.
An assurance register entry is marked RESTRICTED or EMBARGOED and therefore requires repository-visibility review before publication.
The same register-entry identifier appears more than once within or across the assurance registers.
A register entry references an identifier that cannot be resolved in the target register.

context_effect_rules.when: the target register is present and usable but the referenced identifier is absent

allocated_entries.semantic_condition: A critical invariant subject to operational grounding lacks a committed reference in at least one required enforcement or verification list.
An invariant with an affirmative intent classification lacks a committed human authority record.
A residual marked ACCEPTED lacks a committed accepted-by value, acceptance date, or acceptance rationale.
A residual marked RESOLVED lacks a committed resolution note.
A defeater marked RESOLVED, MITIGATED, or WITHDRAWN lacks a committed resolution statement.
The material-change workflow declaration is absent, is not a mapping, or lacks a nonempty system or root value.
The declared material-change workflow root does not identify an existing adopter-controlled regular file or directory contained within the repository.
The declared material-change workflow root contains no readable, nonempty UTF-8 regular entry document within the permitted traversal depth.
Inspecting the declared material-change workflow exceeds an accepted entry-count, document-size, or aggregate-byte limit.
A required root instruction file lacks the visible top-level assurance reading-order block in canonical order.
A component mapping names an invariant that is absent from the active invariant register.
The declared adoption stage is not one of DRAFT, HUMAN_REVIEWED, or CONFORMANT.
A HUMAN_REVIEWED or CONFORMANT adoption retains a template placeholder in a policy artifact or required instruction document.
A critical invariant in a HUMAN_REVIEWED or CONFORMANT adoption lacks a recognized intent classification.
A critical invariant in a CONFORMANT adoption has missing, UNKNOWN, or ACCIDENTAL intent.
A CONFORMANT adoption contains a critical-impact residual whose status remains OPEN.
A CONFORMANT adoption contains a claim whose status is CONTRADICTED.
A CONFORMANT adoption contains a critical invariant whose status is CONTRADICTED.
A non-critical invariant declared by a CONFORMANT adoption is contradicted by the evaluated repository state.
A CONFORMANT adoption lacks required evidence for a critical invariant declared VERIFIED.
A CONFORMANT adoption contains RESTRICTED or EMBARGOED material while repository visibility is public or unestablished.
The recorded approval does not satisfy the authority required for a CONFORMANT adoption.
A review record required by the declared adoption stage is absent.
The declared review record resolves outside the permitted repository boundary.
The recorded review date is later than the evaluation time.
The recorded approval URL is not a syntactically valid absolute HTTP or HTTPS URL with a host, no user information, and a valid optional port.
The recorded approval time is not a valid accepted timestamp.
The recorded approval time is later than the evaluation time.
The issue-integration policy explicitly permits public security issues.
Issue closure is not configured to require the corresponding assurance artifact update.
The mapped system artifact of an archived adoption contains one of the exact shipped section 6.6 template markers.
No durable human review confirms that the declared profile classification matches the project.

authority_refs.locator: closed negative v0.4 inventory for F0072

allocated_entries.semantic_condition: The pinned profile checkout's VERSION resource is not valid UTF-8, contains a second line, or contains a bare carriage return.

default_display.title: Pinned profile version resource is invalid

allocated_entries.meaning: A public check was not run because a required public-check prerequisite ended in a completed blocking failure.
The explicit pull-request base or head revision required for a trusted transition could not be acquired or materialized.
The requested pull-request transition is applicable, but the privileged pull_request_target boundary is not authorized to evaluate untrusted head code or data.
A non-deletion push is a snapshot invocation and has no selected single-pull-request review transition.
A merge-group snapshot has no selected single-PR body or review transition and cannot substitute for that transition.
A manual snapshot run has no authoritative base, head, and PR-body tuple for a single-PR review transition.
A scheduled snapshot run has no authoritative single-PR base, head, and body tuple.
The trusted underlying caller event is unavailable or unrecognized, so transition applicability cannot be classified safely.

authority_refs.locator: unrecognized or unavailable caller event row

allocated_entries.meaning: The target snapshot for a push, including a deletion push, cannot be identified or materialized safely.
A requested adoption declaration could not be acquired as an input, so no policy result may be inferred from its absence.
Assurance evidence required for transition evaluation could not be acquired or materialized; deterministic absence established by a completed check is excluded from this reason.
A required reusable-workflow invocation was absent or hidden by caller conditions or dependencies and therefore could not produce a trusted run.
The authoritative changed-file set required for transition evaluation could not be computed or acquired.

The legacy provider's separately scheduled declared-stage job does not apply to the selected DRAFT-stage compatibility branch; this does not make the adopter stage capability disappear from a canonical plan.
A trusted implementation failure prevented a run or public check from completing safely after preserving already established semantic results.
The optional upstream pin-staleness query could not be completed; this is not evidence that the selected pin is current or stale.
The optional upstream pin-staleness query returned an empty, malformed, or otherwise unusable response.
The declared release tag could not be resolved reliably because tag acquisition or inspection did not complete; deterministically established tag absence is excluded.
A required external provider action failed without a trusted typed semantic result that distinguishes rule failure from provider non-completion.
The authoritative pull-request body bytes required for review-transition routing could not be materialized; an authoritatively empty body is not this reason.
The release-tag binding check does not apply to the accepted unreleased-version branch.
A required provider or reusable-workflow output was missing, malformed, stale, or not bound to its producing typed result.
A required auxiliary process did not provide an accepted typed semantic result; a completed domain test failure must be represented separately rather than hidden by this reason.
The diagnostic process could not durably emit or hand off one complete typed result document.
A provider-side routing-input materialization boundary could not complete within its accepted limit; a safely acquired input rejected by the engine's bounded parser is a finding instead.
The provider lost the assigned runner before all required results and outputs were durably observed.
The provider could not assign an eligible runner to a required job or run.
A required trusted runtime dependency could not be imported or installed before semantic execution.
The trusted provider cancelled the run before every required semantic result was durably produced.
A trusted run, job, or bounded operation exceeded its accepted deadline before completion.
A provider failed to durably publish an already computed result through its required publication boundary; this reason does not define or authenticate a CI status identity.
The trusted checkout destination could not be inspected or reserved because the boundary operation failed; a deterministically occupied path is a finding instead.
The provider rejected or failed to create the required workflow invocation before any repository job could report a result.
The provider did not supply the trusted workflow repository, path, ref, or revision identity required to establish the execution boundary.
A required bounded input file could not be read or materialized as bytes, so its semantic content is unknown.
The required schema registry could not be acquired or loaded, so schema conformance could not be evaluated.
The pinned profile checkout or its trusted resources could not be acquired as an inspectable repository tree.
The project tree required for repository-bound evaluation could not be acquired or traversed safely.
The authoritative base-revision policy could not be acquired, so a base-to-head transition could not be established.
The authoritative assurance diff required for transition routing could not be acquired or materialized.
The provider reported an outcome outside the accepted closed observation vocabulary, so no semantic completion state can be inferred.

rules.assertion: candidate_lifecycle.acceptance_binding is null and no accepted/review-class field exists
catalog_revision is 1 and parent_catalog_revision is null
catalog_id is a valid lowercase urn:uuid and contains no product, package, executable, or acronym token
exactly 36 unique public checks satisfy ADR 0002 grammar and every proposed dependency names one of them
proposed public dependency input edges are acyclic
every semantic_input_role used by a check or reason is registered at top level
allocated finding codes are exactly the contiguous first-revision range F0001 through F0113, unique, and F0000 remains forbidden
every allocated finding has exactly one existing public check owner
every allocated finding fact schema is closed, has no null values, and each required key is declared
every allocated finding references decision_id AAP-V05-DIAGNOSTICS-001 by its own code and requires external acceptance
no unallocated finding or reason placeholder remains in this complete catalog draft
allocated reason codes are exactly the accepted reservations, the closed terminal-family vocabulary, and the additional semantic-mapping non-completion vocabulary
every allocated reason has non-empty exact owner/state tuples and every CHECK owner is registered
PREREQUISITE_FAILED alone requires blocked_by; all other allocated reasons forbid it
every allocated reason detail schema is closed and forbids null
the selected projection contains exactly the seven accepted Phase 0 case IDs
required-satisfied Phase 0 keys project only to SELECTED_CONDITION_SATISFIED; public check completion remains unbound until ADR 0004
trust-path maps two legacy diagnostics to exactly one F0003 semantic instance
implementation_parity_authorized remains false and no implementation or runtime output schema is present
F0002 fixes the immutable relation head_stage < base_stage over DRAFT < HUMAN_REVIEWED < CONFORMANT and its source binding proves the exact same predicate
register bounded-parse failures map to adoption.document-parse and F0005/F0006/F0007/F0008; only actual schema evaluator conditions map to F0036
every authority reference names one exact registered source_id; mutable unrevisioned document paths are rejected
the bound compatibility manifest contains exactly the 33 INTENDED_CHANGE_PENDING finding codes with nonempty rationale, impact, migration, and registered authority locators
the review-only verifier deterministically reproduces and matches the bound normalized source inventory

runtime_consumption_blockers: The terminal family set is closed at 41 families, but v0.4 has no typed runtime projection; runtime_ready remains false.
The 36-check graph is semantic input and is not an ADR 0004 canonical plan.
Provider-observed reasons require a separately accepted provider contract before runtime use.
Implementation parity requires a later exact-byte projection and acceptance sequence; this candidate cannot authorize it.
